title: SetPubKey creates key inconsistency by not invalidating private key when replacing modulus n
problem: When SetPubKey is called on a context that already has a private key (hasPrvKey=true), it replaces the modulus n without clearing hasPrvKey or the private key fields (d, p, q, dP, dQ, qInv). This creates an inconsistent state where the private key fields were computed from the old n but the context now has a new n. Subsequent private key operations (decrypt/sign) will use inconsistent key material, producing incorrect results without any error indication.
